description: Small benchmark for analyzing long-context packing quality and answer drift.

cases.question: Why did the largest context budget degrade the answer?
cases.reference_answer: The largest context budget degraded the answer because peripheral passages diluted the core evidence and the long-budget response added an unsupported instruction-tuning explanation.

context_items.title: Core Failure Note
context_items.text: Core finding: the large prompt packed many peripheral passages, which diluted the core evidence and buried the failure note.

context_items.title: Unsupported Explanation Note
context_items.text: Core finding: the long-budget answer introduced an instruction-tuning explanation that the evidence never stated.

context_items.title: Instruction Tuning Background
context_items.text: Background: instruction tuning became popular across many product teams and changed annotation workflows, but this note is unrelated to the failure analysis.

context_items.title: Benchmark Averages
context_items.text: Background: long-context benchmarks often report average accuracy, latency, and hardware settings for unrelated studies.

context_items.title: Retrieval Stack Notes
context_items.text: Background: retrieval stacks may include caching, metadata filters, and synthetic documents that do not explain this failure.

answer_snapshots.answer: The large budget hurt quality because peripheral passages diluted the core evidence.
The largest budget degraded the answer because peripheral passages diluted the core evidence.
The largest budget degraded the answer because instruction tuning was removed and the model forgot how to follow prompts.

cases.question: Why did the smallest budget miss the evaluation caveat?
cases.reference_answer: The smallest budget missed the evaluation caveat because the caveat passage about multilingual tail failures was trimmed out of context, but the medium budget restored it.

context_items.title: Average Summary
context_items.text: Relevant summary: the smallest budget preserved only the benchmark average summary and omitted the caveat passage.

context_items.title: Tail Failure Caveat
context_items.text: Relevant caveat: benchmark averages hid multilingual tail failures, and this caveat was trimmed out at the smallest budget.

context_items.title: Dashboard Export Notes
context_items.text: Noise: dashboard color settings and report export notes.

answer_snapshots.answer: The smallest budget kept only the benchmark average summary and missed the caveat passage.
The medium budget restored the caveat that benchmark averages hid multilingual tail failures.
